postData.contentHtml:
A seismic shift is underway at the iconic news program '60 Minutes', as CBS News leader Bari Weiss implements a 'new approach' that has triggered widespread internal turmoil. The changes, which include the replacement of an executive producer, have not been met with universal approval, sparking talent revolts and notable departures.

At the heart of the controversy is veteran correspondent Scott Pelley, who reportedly accused Weiss of 'murdering' the program during a heated staff meeting. Pelley also reportedly criticized new executive producer Nick Bilton, a former investigative tech journalist and filmmaker, questioning his qualifications and the direction of the show. The meeting, intended to introduce Bilton to the staff, instead became a forum for discontent.

Among the significant changes, former executive producer Tanya Simon was terminated. Additionally, reporters Cecilia Vega and Sharyn Alfonsi have also parted ways with '60 Minutes', with Vega alleging she was 'fired' and citing 'efforts to insert political bias into our stories' in recent months. These departures and accusations paint a picture of a newsroom grappling with fundamental shifts in editorial vision.

Weiss's leadership at CBS News and the subsequent overhaul at '60 Minutes' follow Paramount Skydance's acquisition of her news and opinion site, The Free Press, in October 2024. This context suggests a broader strategic move to reshape the network's journalistic identity. While the internal reaction has been sharp, social media sentiment regarding these changes is reportedly 'mostly positive, with some debate,' indicating a divided public opinion on the shake-up at the venerable news institution.

The future of '60 Minutes' under this 'new approach' remains to be seen, but it's clear that the changes initiated by Bari Weiss are redefining the landscape of one of television's most respected news programs.
postData.title: Turmoil at '60 Minutes': Bari Weiss's 'New Approach' Sparks Outcry and Departures
postData.summary: CBS News leader Bari Weiss's 'new approach' at '60 Minutes' has led to significant internal upheaval, including executive producer replacements and a strong rebuke from veteran correspondent Scott Pelley.

sources.title: Scott Pelley Claimed CBS News Boss Is 'Murdering' 60 Minutes During Heated Staff Meeting: Report
